finding: The CLAUDE.md excerpt rule defines what to extract (## Conventions span capped at 50 lines, else first 50 lines) and what to do when CLAUDE.md does not exist, but never says WHERE tp looks for CLAUDE.md. Reasonable implementations diverge observably: CWD/CLAUDE.md, git repo root, or the task file's directory produce different Project Context content (or omit the section entirely) whenever tp audit is invoked from a subdirectory or the task file lives away from the root. The spec is elsewhere explicit about path resolution (§8.6 spells out cleaning/absolutization; §6.2 pins the gate's working directory to the task file's directory), so an implementer and the author of TestGenerateAuditPrompts_CLAUDEmdOnlyForMaintainability must guess the lookup path.

resolved.evidence: Round-5 revision applied to spec/0.23.0.md §3.4: CLAUDE.md lookup path fixed deterministically — task-file directory first, git repository root fallback, omission when absent in both

suggestion: Add one sentence to §3.4 fixing the location, e.g. 'CLAUDE.md is resolved in the directory containing the resolved task file, falling back to the git repository root when absent there' (or any single deterministic rule), so the omission case is well-defined regardless of invocation directory.
